behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is handler+signature-verifier+queue handoff: invalid signature must both map to 401 and suppress enqueue. A pure unit test of only HMAC compare can miss wiring bugs where enqueue still happens. Rejected higher level: not System because no real OS/network/subprocess behavior is required; in-process HTTP harness and queue spy can reproduce the failure. Not Agentic/Workflow: no AI output.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the HTTP handler with real signature-check module and a fake/spied queue adapter. Send a request with mismatched `X-Signature-256` vs raw body; assert HTTP 401 and assert queue `enqueue` was never called.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is cross-module ordering/state handoff between HTTP layer and queue persistence call. Unit is too low because ordering bug appears at module boundary. Rejected System: ordering can be proven with in-process app + controllable queue adapter (deferred resolve) without real network/OS primitives.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler with a queue implementation whose `write` resolves only after a test-controlled signal. Issue valid webhook request, verify response is not sent before `write` resolves; then resolve `write` and assert 200 is returned.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is orchestration between ingress handler, queueing, and downstream processor trigger. Need module wiring to confirm ack decouples from processing completion. Rejected System: real network/OS not required; an in-process app with a blocking downstream stub catches the bug. No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Configure downstream processor mock to block (never resolve) while queue write succeeds quickly. Send valid webhook and assert response is returned promptly (200) even though processor promise remains pending.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets wall-clock SLA under concurrency and backlog pressure. Real runtime scheduling/timing effects are the failure boundary; heavy concurrency bugs can be hidden by in-process fakes or mocked timers. Rejected Integration based on behavior text 'within 3 seconds wall-clock time' and 'slow and concurrent' which requires real timing execution characteristics.
behaviors.test_description: Start the service process with real HTTP endpoint and queue/worker configuration. Generate earlier events that force slow concurrent downstream processing, then send a new valid webhook and measure end-to-end HTTP latency; assert response time <3s.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Dedup correctness depends on handler-to-storage state handoff across requests; unit logic alone can miss persistence/wiring mistakes. Rejected System: behavior text does not require OS/network/subprocess semantics; duplicate suppression can be validated with in-process app plus real dedup repository module.
behaviors.test_description: Issue two valid webhook requests with the same event ID through the handler wired to the real dedup/persistence module (test DB/file allowed). Assert first request writes once, second request does not create a second queue record.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is mapping queue-capacity signal/error into HTTP contract (status+header). This is module interaction; unit of one mapper function is insufficient if wiring drops header/status. Rejected System: no real OS/network/subprocess required; queue-full condition can be produced by test double or bounded test queue.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler with queue adapter that returns a `full`/capacity error. Send valid request and assert HTTP 503 plus `Retry-After` header is present with expected value/format.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: This targets queue component behavior with real persistence module and configured storage limits; integration with local DB/filesystem is sufficient per level defs. Rejected Unit because requirement says 'persistent queue actually enforces' (not just algorithm in isolation). Rejected System because no external OS/network/subprocess behavior is inherently required beyond local persistence integration.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real persistent queue against a local test store with capacity N. Enqueue N events successfully, then enqueue N+1 and assert explicit rejection/error indicating capacity exceeded.
